info.title: LumenLoop API
info.description: REST access to the LumenLoop Stellar-ecosystem content platform. Every operation is a tool call: POST /tools/{name} with JSON arguments (base URL https://api.lumenloop.com/v1). The same tools are available over MCP at mcp.lumenloop.com. Authenticate with `Authorization: Bearer <llmcp_ API key or OAuth access token>`. Start at the base URL (index), /tools (catalog), /docs (guide).

apiKey.description: LumenLoop API key (starts with `llmcp_`). Tiers: guest < partner < read < write < manager < admin. Contact LumenLoop for a key.

oauth.description: OAuth access token from the LumenLoop authorization server. Discovery: /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource. New OAuth identities default to the guest tier.

post.summary: Find specific passages inside long AV transcripts via per-chunk embedding similarity
post.description:
Find specific passages inside long AV transcripts via per-chunk embedding similarity. Use this when you want to quote what was said, not just "which video". Returns the parent video metadata plus the matched chunk text and its start offset.

**When to use:** Find WHERE in videos/podcasts/spaces a topic is discussed — returns AI summaries of the matching passages plus an opaque ordering offset (NOT a playback timestamp). Use when you need "the part of the talk where they discuss X".

**Returns:** Array of AV items with AI summary, created_at (the recording's date — use it to judge and cite how recent the talk is), start_offset (an opaque transcript chunk offset for ordering passages within a recording — NOT playback seconds; do not render it as a timestamp or build a deep-link from it), and link. Transcript text itself is never returned — cite the link + the passage summary.

query.description: Topic or phrase to match against transcript passages

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

results.description: Array of AV items: AI summary, start_offset (opaque transcript chunk offset, not seconds), link.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

post.summary: Find articles, AV content, events, and tweets that are semantically related to a project
post.description:
Find articles, AV content, events, and tweets that are semantically related to a project. Uses embedding similarity to find cross-domain content that DISCUSSES the project. Supports optional date/source/type filters for scoped research.

**When to use:** All published content related to ONE project, keyed BY SLUG (resolve a name to a slug with search_directory first). For an open-ended topic use search_content_semantic; for entity-name mentions (incl. tokens/orgs) where you only have a name use find_content_by_entity.

**Returns:** Content grouped by type with AI summaries + source links + date, filtered to the project, recency-aware so recent coverage (incidents, launches) surfaces alongside canonical content. response_format 'concise' (external default) omits long_summary; pass 'detailed' to include it.

limit.description: Max results per content type (default 20). Effective rows = limit × number of types — keep it small and narrow `types` instead of raising it.

response_format.description: 'concise' omits long_summary from each row (smaller payloads); 'detailed' keeps it. Default: detailed.

date_start.description: ISO date/timestamp lower bound (inclusive). For articles, applies to COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at) by default to avoid stale re-scrape pollution.

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

sources.description: Filter by source. For articles: domain. For AV: channel. For events: platform. For tweets: user_screen_name.

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

articles.description: Related articles with AI summaries + source links.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.

post.summary: Find articles, AV, events, governance proposals, and SCF submissions that mention a specific named entity
post.description:
Find articles, AV, events, governance proposals, and SCF submissions that mention a specific named entity. Uses LLM-extracted entities (project/person/organization/token) from content_entities. Case-insensitive match on entity_name or exact match on normalized_slug. Use this when you want exact entity grounding, not semantic similarity.

**When to use:** Find content mentioning a NAMED entity (project, organization, token) by case-insensitive name match with LLM-confidence filtering — works even when the entity has no directory slug. If you have a slug, prefer find_content_about_project.

**Returns:** Array of content items with the matched entity, confidence score, and content metadata.

entity.description: Entity name (case-insensitive) or normalized slug. Examples: "Aquarius", "aquarius", "StellarOrg".

entity_type.description: Restrict to a specific entity type

content_type.description: Restrict to a specific content type

min_confidence.description: Minimum LLM confidence to include (0.0-1.0). Default 0.5 filters out weak matches.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

results.description: Array of content items with matched entity, confidence, metadata.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.summary: Find projects semantically similar to a given project using embedding cosine similarity
post.description:
Find projects semantically similar to a given project using embedding cosine similarity. More accurate than tag/category matching.

**When to use:** Find projects similar to a known project (by slug) via embedding similarity — better than tag/category matching. For keyword search use search_directory.

**Returns:** Array of projects ranked by cosine similarity: slug, title, description, similarity score.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

results.description: Array of similar projects: slug, title, description, similarity score.

post.summary: Search SCF (Stellar Community Fund) submissions by embedding similarity
post.description:
Search SCF (Stellar Community Fund) submissions by embedding similarity. Pass a natural-language query for topic/category discovery or an existing submission slug to find similar prior proposals. Returns title, description, category, round, award_type, the linked_project_slug (the primary directory project, if any), and linked_project_slugs (the full set of directory projects this submission credits).

**When to use:** Explore SCF submissions by topic (natural-language query) or find proposals similar to an existing one (slug). Query and slug are mutually exclusive.

**Returns:** Array of submissions ranked by similarity, with round/category/funding metadata, linked_project_slug (the primary credited directory project) and linked_project_slugs (the full set of credited directory projects).

query.description: Natural-language query (e.g. "ZK proofs for compliance"). Mutually exclusive with `slug`.

slug.description: Existing SCF submission slug (e.g. "recsAQHCghw9HIUvF") — returns semantically similar submissions. Mutually exclusive with `query`.

round.description: Restrict to a specific SCF round (e.g. "SCF #29")

category.description: Restrict to a specific category (e.g. "Developer Tooling")

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

results.description: Array of similar submissions with round/category/funding, linked_project_slug (primary credited project) and linked_project_slugs (full credited set).

post.summary: Get full details of a single document by ID from any collection
post.description:
Get full details of a single document by ID from any collection. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av" — they map to the same table.

**When to use:** Fetch one document by collection + numeric id, after a list/search tool gave you the id.

**Returns:** One document. External callers get the summary projection (title, url, summary, long_summary, tags, dates) — full body text and transcripts are not exposed; cite the returned url instead.

collection.description: Content collection (videos and av both target the av table)

post.summary: Get details of a project from the ecosystem directory by slug
post.description:
Get details of a project from the ecosystem directory by slug. Pass compact=true to get a slim writer-friendly shape (~500 chars: slug, title, short description, category, tags, twitter_handles, website, other_names) — recommended for writer agents that just need to verify a slug and read @ handles. Default returns the full row (~8k chars including conflict tracking, source values, field policy).

**When to use:** Fetch one project BY SLUG. If you only have a name, resolve it to a slug with search_directory first — a guessed slug will 404. Pass compact=true when you only need identity fields for writing/citation (cuts ~95% of the payload).

**Returns:** One project object: slug, title, description, category, tags, links (website, x, github, …), based_in, operating_region, SCF awards. Internal-only fields (source values, conflicts, hidden links) are never included for external callers.

compact.description: Return slim writer shape instead of full row. Cuts ~95% of payload size.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.summary: Given a content item (article/av/event/research) return the directory projects it mentions, via the content_projects jun
post.description:
Given a content item (article/av/event/research) return the directory projects it mentions, via the content_projects junction.

**When to use:** You have a CONTENT item (article/av/event/research id) and want the ecosystem projects it mentions — the "Mentioned projects" on lumenloop.com. The reverse direction (project → content) is find_content_about_project.

**Returns:** Array of mentioned projects with public info (slug, title, description, category).

content_type.description: Content type. Canonical singular: article | av | event | research | proposal (plural/alias accepted).

post.summary: Get Stellar Community Fund submissions linked to a project, including the full application/proposal text (markdown: sect
post.description:
Get Stellar Community Fund submissions linked to a project, including the full application/proposal text (markdown: sections, milestones, deliverables, budget detail), funding amount, award status, round, and category. Searches by slug or project name — pass at least one.

**When to use:** Get Stellar Community Fund submissions linked to a specific project. Pass slug (resolve a name via search_directory) or a fuzzy name. For topic-based exploration across all submissions use find_similar_scf_submissions.

**Returns:** { count, submissions: [...] } — each: title, round, category, award_type, funding amounts, description, the full application/proposal text (markdown), links, linked_project_slug (the primary credited directory project) and linked_project_slugs (the full set of directory projects this submission credits).

slug.description: Project slug (preferred — resolve names via search_directory first)

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

post.summary: Browse/enumerate ONE collection by structured criteria — status, source, date, time period (upcoming/past events) — with
post.description:
Browse/enumerate ONE collection by structured criteria — status, source, date, time period (upcoming/past events) — with sorting and pagination. Its optional "search" is the SAME exact case-insensitive SUBSTRING match on title and URL as search_documents (not body text, not meaning). For conceptual or question-phrased discovery, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer, use search_content_semantic instead. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av".

**When to use:** Browse/page through a content collection (articles, events, videos/av, research) with filters and sorting. For text matching use search_documents; for meaning-based discovery use search_content_semantic.

**Returns:** Page of document summaries plus pagination info. External callers get summary projection: title, url, slug, summary, long_summary, tags, dates — never full article bodies or AV transcripts.

collection.description: Content collection to query (videos and av both target the av table)

period.description: Events only: filter by time period

sort.description: Sort field. Articles: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, domain, source. Events: start_at, processed_at, created_at. Videos: processed_at, publishing_date, created_at, source. Jobs: updated_at, last_seen_at, published_at, created_at.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

items.description: Document summaries: title, url, slug, summary, long_summary, tags, dates.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.summary: List research drafts with optional filters
post.description:
List research drafts with optional filters. Used by drafter agents for dedup ("has anything similar been drafted lately?") and by agents needing to reference prior work. Returns preview fields only — call get_document(collection="research", id) for full body.

**When to use:** Browse LumenLoop's own published research/editorial pieces (previews) — id, title, summary, links.

**Returns:** Array of research previews: id, title, format, status, summary, created_at — not full bodies.

format.description: Filter by format ('sources' = partner source packs, 'structured' = partner schema-fills).

source.description: Filter by source prefix match (e.g. "agent:" for all agent-authored).

since.description: ISO date/timestamp; only rows with created_at >= this.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

research.description: Research previews: id, title, format, status, summary, created_at.

post.summary: Search all content semantically
post.description:
Search all content semantically. Embeds the query text and finds similar articles, AV, events, and tweets via pgvector cosine distance. Use this (not search_documents/list_documents) whenever the query is conceptual or phrased as a question, or when you do not know which collection holds the answer; reserve search_documents for exact title/URL keyword lookup. Supports date/source/type filters — use these for scoped research queries like "find Stellar DeFi content from March" or "what did SDF post about privacy".

**When to use:** Meaning-based search across ALL published content types at once (articles, AV, events, research) — the default discovery tool for "what has been said about X". For exact-term lookup in one collection use search_documents; for content about one specific project use find_content_about_project. For "latest/current" ecosystem facts (protocol version, program/fund status, recent incidents or launches) prefer this over live web — results are dated and the corpus updates continuously; cite the date. Use web only for facts the corpus cannot hold, e.g. upstream package/registry pins (crates.io, npm, GitHub release tags).

**Returns:** Results grouped by content type, each with AI summary, source url, date, and similarity score — never full text. Cite the returned url. response_format 'concise' (external default) omits long_summary; pass 'detailed' to include it.

limit.description: Max results per content type (default 20). Effective rows = limit × number of types — keep it small and narrow `types` instead of raising it.

response_format.description: 'concise' omits long_summary from each row (smaller payloads); 'detailed' keeps it. Default: detailed.

date_start.description: ISO date/timestamp lower bound (inclusive). For articles, applies to COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at) by default to avoid stale re-scrape pollution.

date_field.description: For articles only: force a specific date field. Default is COALESCE(publishing_date, created_at).

sources.description: Filter by source. For articles: domain. For AV: channel. For events: platform. For tweets: user_screen_name.

types.description: Restrict to specific content types. Default: all 9 (knowledge = internal dated notes). Narrow this for smaller, faster results — fanning out across all types multiplies the row count.

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

articles.description: Article hits: AI summary, source url, date, similarity.

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

403.description: Credential tier below the tool's required tier.

post.summary: Search the Stellar ecosystem directory for projects by name, description, or category (substring match)
post.description:
Search the Stellar ecosystem directory for projects by name, description, or category (substring match). If a multi-word or conceptual query matches no substring, it automatically falls back to semantic project discovery (response carries match_mode: "semantic") so you still get the closest projects.

**When to use:** Start here to find a Stellar ecosystem project by name, keyword, or category when you do not know its slug. Substring match, but a multi-word/conceptual query that hits no substring auto-falls back to semantic project discovery, so you can also use it for "what RWA/wallet/oracle projects exist" style asks. For "projects like X" use find_similar_projects_semantic; for content ABOUT a project use find_content_about_project.

**Returns:** { count, projects: [...] } — each project: slug, title, description, category, tags. When the substring match is empty and the semantic fallback fires, the response also carries match_mode: "semantic" + a note, and rows include a similarity score. Take the slug into get_project for full details.

query.description: Search query (substring match on title, description, other_names, and category)

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

projects.description: project matches: slug, title, description, category, tags, website

text.description: Present instead of the data fields when the tool reports a plain-text message.

post.summary: Exact keyword lookup: case-insensitive SUBSTRING match on title and URL only (NOT body text, NOT meaning) within ONE col
post.description:
Exact keyword lookup: case-insensitive SUBSTRING match on title and URL only (NOT body text, NOT meaning) within ONE collection. Use when you already know wording that appears in a title or URL (e.g. find the article about a known announcement). For conceptual or question-phrased queries use search_content_semantic; to browse a collection by status/date/source use list_documents. The av collection accepts either "videos" or "av".

**When to use:** Exact/keyword match on title or URL within one collection (e.g. find the article about a known announcement). For conceptual queries ("content about cross-border payments") use search_content_semantic.

**Returns:** Array of matching document summaries (same external projection as list_documents).

collection.description: Content collection to search (videos and av both target the av table)

200.description: Tool result in the standard envelope.

items.description: Matching document summaries (same projection as list_documents).

get.description: Public. A presented credential personalizes it (availability flags, internal tools for internal tiers). Optional ?tier= filter.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

get.description: Public, no auth. Every deploy-worthy API/MCP change, newest first. Filter with ?since=YYYY-MM-DD (entries dated on or after that day; 400 on malformed dates) and ?breaking=true (only changes an integration could parse-depend on).

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: YYYY-MM-DD — only entries dated on or after this day (inclusive).

200.description: Changelog entries, newest first.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

get.summary: Agent Skill playbooks: catalog of both sets
get.description: Public. Lists the Stellar-ecosystem MCP skills (public set) and the REST API partner skills (partner set) with availability flags for the presented credential.

200.description: Skill catalog + archive links.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

get.summary: One skill: every file inline (SKILL.md + reference/)
get.description: Public for the public set; partner-set skills require the partner tier (403 insufficient_scope otherwise).

200.description: Skill files inline.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

403.description: Partner-set skill without partner tier.

get.summary: Prebuilt zip of a skill set
get.description: set=public (Stellar-ecosystem MCP skills, no auth) or set=partner (REST API skills, partner tier).

post.description: Returns the plaintext key exactly once. Minted keys are partner-tier only — self-service can never raise a tier. Max 10 active keys per account.

post.summary: Ask an admin to raise your budget
post.description: The relationship path — a human reviews and applies the change. Distinct from POST /billing/topup, which credits your account instantly via x402. At most one open request per account (409 while one is pending). kind="quota" asks to raise the recurring monthly allowance; kind="credits" asks for a one-time allocation.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

409.description: You already have an open budget request.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

200.description: Config or {configured:false}.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

403.description: Not a partner credential.

put.summary: Register/replace the research-run callback; returns the signing secret once

200.description: Registered; data.secret shown once.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

400.description: URL rejected (not https / not publicly routable).

403.description: Not a partner credential.

200.description: Removed.

post.summary: Top up allocated research credits — pay USDC on Stellar (x402)
post.description: Paid via the x402 protocol: the first call returns HTTP 402 with payment requirements in the `payment-required` response header (base64 JSON; exact scheme, USDC on Stellar). Pay with an x402-capable client and retry with the `X-PAYMENT` header; on settlement the amount is credited to your billing account and the settlement transaction is returned in the `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header. Requires a billing account on your credential (409 otherwise). 503 when payments are not enabled on this deployment.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: USD amount to credit (1–500; default set by the server).

200.description: Payment settled; credit recorded.

data.description: Tool result. Parsed JSON when the tool emits JSON, { text } otherwise.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
Actionable next step for the caller.

402.description: Payment required — x402 terms in the payment-required header.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

409.description: Credential has no billing account to credit.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.

503.description: x402 payments not configured on this deployment.

hint.description: Actionable next step for the caller.
